data.body:
EXPERIMENT (Sonnet-scored simulation on 31 labeled code-review findings, 3 epochs; prior art: SonarQube MQR, Semgrep/SpotBugs confidence-gate, SSVC tree, CodeScene hotspots, mutation-testing, FMEA S/O/D):
- RELIABILITY: atomic sub-questions answered with ~1.0 inter-epoch agreement => decomposition is reliable. GOOD.
- DISCRIMINATION: signal concentrates in DEGREE/magnitude questions — maintainability/fragility severity (AUC 0.775), correctness/contract (0.70), test-guards-nothing (0.70). The yes/no DETECTION + nit-gate questions SATURATE (silent-failure, escapes-automation both AUC 0.50; deficiency-vs-preference 0.525 — Sonnet calls 95% of nits 'deficiencies' too).
- CAVEAT driving saturation: scored from finding-TEXT-only, which advocates for the concern, so yes/no 'is-this-real/severe' collapses to yes. Degree questions survive the bias. => the detection/value sub-questions MUST be embedded in the verifier with DIFF grounding to work; a post-hoc text classifier can't.
- OLD model AUC 0.827 (ranks OK) but compresses all scores to 0.40-0.67 => the real defect is CALIBRATION (absolute scale), not ranking. No new-axis model beat it FROM TEXT (unfair: old attrs were diff-grounded).
CONCLUSION: rework = (1) RE-ANCHOR the code scale so a high-maintainability finding reaches ~0.8+ absolute (the core fix); (2) lean sub-questions, DEGREE-framed, embedded in the verifier (diff-grounded): correctness-severity, maintainability/fragility-severity, security-severity -> MAX (SonarQube per-quality, do NOT average); test-effectiveness (untested-new-behavior / test-guards-nothing) as a landmine AMPLIFIER; confidence + deficiency-vs-preference as GATES not summands (Semgrep/SonarQube confidence-gate, Google nit:). DROP reversibility+likelihood (they suppressed maintainability). (3) VALIDATE with a diff-grounded A/B before trust — the text-only sim cannot confirm a win.
